message:
test(fuzz): add SchemaId::hash injectivity / collision-space target (resolves k1bwk)

Adds fuzz_schema_id_hash to probe the post-mzi9x length-prefixed
encoding's collision resistance across arbitrary
(namespace, name, version) tuples.

Properties asserted:

  1. Determinism — re-hashing the same SchemaId yields the same digest.
  2. Injectivity — equal tuples ⟺ equal 32-byte hashes.  Bypasses
     SchemaId::new / try_new validation by setting public fields
     directly, modeling an attacker who constructs a SchemaId literal
     in code or via serde and embeds reserved separators (':' / '@')
     to try to alias a schema's cryptographic identity onto another.
  3. Validation parity — SchemaId::try_new accepts iff neither
     component contains a reserved separator, and any accepted
     tuple hashes identically to its direct-field counterpart.

Complements the hand-picked schema_hash_vectors.json golden cases by
fuzzing the entire collision search space, including length-boundary
attacks the original concat-encoding (`namespace':'name'@'version`)
was vulnerable to.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -125,6 +125,13 @@ test = false
 doc = false
 bench = false
 
+[[bin]]
+name = "fuzz_schema_id_hash"
+path = "fuzz_targets/schema_id_hash.rs"
+test = false
+doc = false
+bench = false
+
 [[bin]]
 name = "fuzz_version_negotiation_handshake"
 path = "fuzz_targets/version_negotiation_handshake.rs"
